audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in ldapdelete.c are guarded: `ld` is always non-NULL because `tool_conn_setup(0,...)` calls `exit()` on failure; `ldap_get_dn()` result is checked before use (L405); `res` is valid before `ldap_parse_result` because the loop only breaks on rc>0; and the single `ber_memfree(dn)` call where `dn==NULL` (L408) is safe because `ber_memfree_x()` explicitly guards against NULL input (memory.c:128-130). The `entries>0` guard ensures `ldap_first_entry` is never called with a NULL `res`.
